mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent during tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity and output growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of expansionary periods across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust employment growth and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction with Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 225.0 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized consumer and aggregate demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to yield positive 20-day SPX returns with a win rate below 45% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle phases signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observed cases.
